This report presents investigative leads based on transaction pattern analysis. It does not establish guilt — all findings should be independently verified before action.

Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 24,576,746.11 moved across the accounts in this case, with Rs 10,507,045.92 coming from outside the suspect network.
  • Sarita Deepa, account number 17496072039317, sent out Rs 11,211,289.04, making it the largest source of funds.
  • The money was routed through 3 intermediary accounts that received and quickly passed on the funds.
  • Sarita Deepa's account sent money to 840 different accounts.
  • Sarita Deepa's account received money from 352 different accounts.
  • Rs 12,815,676.22 of the incoming funds were used for onward transfers or withdrawals, while Rs 1,689,793.78 could not be fully traced.
  • The first account to investigate is Sarita Deepa, account number 17496072039317, due to its suspicious behaviors in this case.

5 · Money-Flow Graphs

Case Overview Graph

The graph shows 2 clusters, with cluster_001 being the most notable, containing 87 accounts and 7120 edges, and having a total score of 22291.0949. Cluster_001 also exhibits the most patterns, with 17 different pattern_ids. An investigator should take note of the significant difference in activity between cluster_001 and cluster_002, which has only 1 account and 0 edges. Account Interconnection Graph

This static exhibit is filtered for legibility: it shows the material account-to-account relationships and only the most evidential external counterparties, while the full-density network remains available in the UI graph data.

Suspicious Activity Timeline

The graph shows a timeline of suspicious activity from 2018-01-06 to 2025-12-31, covering 22671 transactions. The most notable figure is the total number of transactions, with 5 flagged and excluded. An investigator should take note of the large number of transactions over the nearly 8-year time span. The high volume of transactions may indicate a pattern worth further investigation.

Fraud-Pattern Summary

The graph shows various fraud patterns with 'Duplicate Verification' being the most frequent at 744 occurrences, followed by 'Shared Upi Identifiers' at 143, and 'Money Trail Tracing' at 100. An investigator should note the high frequency of 'Duplicate Verification' and 'Shared Upi Identifiers' as potential indicators of fraudulent activity. The presence of 72 'Low Value Account Testing' instances and 14 'Suspicious Account Ranking' instances also warrants further investigation.

Generated by the Survey Corps Investigation Engine for CIDECODE · Run run_20260705_065646 · 05 July 2026, 07:04. All findings are investigative leads requiring independent verification.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
